title: Multi-Head Attention
content:
# Interview Prep: MHA

## Q1: Why is the model dimension d_model divided by the number of heads h (i.e. d_k = d_model / h) in production implementations?

### Standard Answer
Dividing $d_{model}$ by $h$ ensures that the computational cost of Multi-Head Attention is identical to that of single-head attention with full dimensionality. The total parameter count and operations are kept constant, while allowing the model to attend to information from different representation subspaces simultaneously.
